TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi abstract beforefieldinit AssemblyBuilder extends System.Reflection.Assembly
Public MustInherit Class AssemblyBuilder Inherits Assembly
public ref class AssemblyBuilder abstract : System::Reflection::Assembly

Docs.summary: Defines and represents a dynamic assembly.

example.format:
The following code example shows how to define and use a dynamic assembly. The example assembly contains one type, `MyDynamicType`, that has a private field, a property that gets and sets the private field, constructors that initialize the private field, and a method that multiplies a user-supplied number by the private field value and returns the result.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/Overview/24895.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="fsharp" source="~/snippets/fsharp/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/Overview/24895.fs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/Overview/24895.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: .method familyhidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
Docs.remarks: This constructor is invoked by derived classes.

Docs.summary: Adds an existing resource file to this assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property CodeBase As String
public: virtual property System::String ^ CodeBase { System::String ^ get(); };

Attribute.AttributeName: [System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFiles("This member throws an exception for assemblies embedded in a single-file app")]
[<System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresAssemblyFiles("This member throws an exception for assemblies embedded in a single-file app")>]

[System.Obsolete("Assembly.CodeBase and Assembly.EscapedCodeBase are only included for .NET Framework compatibility. Use Assembly.Location instead.", DiagnosticId="SYSLIB0012", UrlFormat="https://aka.ms/dotnet-warnings/{0}")]

Docs.summary: Gets the location of the assembly, as specified originally (such as in an object).
Docs.value: The location of the assembly, as specified originally.
Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported.
The caller does not have the required permission.

Docs.summary: Defines a dynamic assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: public static System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder DefineDynamicAssembly (System.Reflection.AssemblyName name, System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilderAccess access);

.method public static hidebysig class System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder DefineDynamicAssembly(class System.Reflection.AssemblyName name, valuetype System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilderAccess access) cil managed
Public Shared Function DefineDynamicAssembly (name As AssemblyName, access As AssemblyBuilderAccess) As AssemblyBuilder

Attribute.AttributeName: [System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCode("Defining a dynamic assembly requires dynamic code.")]
[<System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCode("Defining a dynamic assembly requires dynamic code.")>]

Docs.param: The name of the assembly.
The access rights of the assembly.
Docs.summary: Defines a dynamic assembly that has the specified name and access rights.
Docs.returns: An object that represents the new assembly.
Docs.remarks: .NET 6+ only: If the property is null, then the assembly is created in the of the function that calls DefineDynamicAssembly. Otherwise, it's created with the specified in .

MemberSignature.Value: public static System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder DefineDynamicAssembly (System.Reflection.AssemblyName name, System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilderAccess access, System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder>? assemblyAttributes);

.method public static hidebysig class System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder DefineDynamicAssembly(class System.Reflection.AssemblyName name, valuetype System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilderAccess access, class System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<class System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder> assemblyAttributes) cil managed
Public Shared Function DefineDynamicAssembly (name As AssemblyName, access As AssemblyBuilderAccess, assemblyAttributes As IEnumerable(Of CustomAttributeBuilder)) As AssemblyBuilder

public: static System::Reflection::Emit::AssemblyBuilder ^ DefineDynamicAssembly(System::Reflection::AssemblyName ^ name, System::Reflection::Emit::AssemblyBuilderAccess access, System::Collections::Generic::IEnumerable<System::Reflection::Emit::CustomAttributeBuilder ^> ^ assemblyAttributes);

Attribute.AttributeName: [System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCode("Defining a dynamic assembly requires dynamic code.")]
[<System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresDynamicCode("Defining a dynamic assembly requires dynamic code.")>]

Docs.param: The name of the assembly.
The access rights of the assembly.
A collection that contains the attributes of the assembly.
Docs.summary: Defines a dynamic assembly that has the specified name, access rights, and attributes.
Docs.returns: An object that represents the new assembly.
Docs.remarks: .NET 6+ only: If the property is null, then the assembly is created in the of the function that calls DefineDynamicAssembly. Otherwise, it's created with the specified in .

Docs.summary: Defines a dynamic module in this assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig instance class System.Reflection.Emit.ModuleBuilder DefineDynamicModule(string name) cil managed
Public Function DefineDynamicModule (name As String) As ModuleBuilder

Docs.param: The name of the dynamic module.
Docs.summary: Defines a named transient dynamic module in this assembly.
Docs.returns: A representing the defined dynamic module.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
[Multi-module assemblies are not supported](/dotnet/core/porting/net-framework-tech-unavailable#loading-multi-module-assemblies). Only one dynamic module can be defined in an assembly.
The defined dynamic module is transient. The dynamic module is not saved, even if the parent dynamic assembly was created with <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilderAccess.RunAndSave>.
> [!NOTE] > To suppress optimizations when debugging dynamic modules, apply the <xref:System.Diagnostics.DebuggableAttribute> attribute to the dynamic assembly before calling <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder.DefineDynamicModule*>. Create an instance of <xref:System.Diagnostics.DebuggableAttribute> with the <xref:System.Diagnostics.DebuggableAttribute.DebuggingModes.DisableOptimizations> flag and apply it using the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder.SetCustomAttribute*> method. The attribute must be applied to the dynamic assembly. It has no effect if applied to the module.
## Examples
The code example below demonstrates how to create a transient dynamic module using `DefineDynamicModule`.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/DefineDynamicModule/source.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/DefineDynamicModule/source.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception:
begins with white space.
-or-
The length of is zero.
-or-
The length of is greater than the system-defined maximum length.
is .
A dynamic module has already been defined in this assembly.
The caller does not have the required permission.

The assembly for default symbol writer cannot be loaded.
-or-
The type that implements the default symbol writer interface cannot be found.

MemberSignature.Value: .method familyhidebysig newslot virtual instance class System.Reflection.Emit.ModuleBuilder DefineDynamicModuleCore(string name) cil managed
Protected MustOverride Function DefineDynamicModuleCore (name As String) As ModuleBuilder

Docs.param: The name of the dynamic module.
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, defines a dynamic module in this assembly.
Docs.returns: A representing the defined dynamic module.
Docs.remarks: Only one module per assembly is supported.

Docs.summary: Defines a standalone managed resource for this assembly.
Defines an unmanaged resource for this assembly.

Docs.returns: if equals the type and value of this instance; otherwise, .

public: virtual property System::String ^ FullName { System::String ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the display name of the current dynamic assembly.
Docs.value: The display name of the dynamic assembly.

remarks.format: ## Remarks See <xref:System.Reflection.AssemblyName> for a description of the format of the display name of an assembly.

Docs.summary: Returns custom attributes that have been applied to the current .

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig virtual instance object[] GetCustomAttributes(bool inherit) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetCustomAttributes (inherit As Boolean) As Object()

Docs.param: This argument is ignored for objects of this type.
Docs.summary: Returns all the custom attributes that have been applied to the current .
Docs.returns: An array that contains the custom attributes; the array is empty if there are no attributes.

MemberSignature.Value: public override object[] GetCustomAttributes (Type attributeType, bool inherit);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance object[] GetCustomAttributes(class System.Type attributeType, bool inherit) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetCustomAttributes (attributeType As Type, inherit As Boolean) As Object()
public: override cli::array <System::Object ^> ^ GetCustomAttributes(Type ^ attributeType, bool inherit);

Docs.param: The base type from which attributes derive.
This argument is ignored for objects of this type.
Docs.summary: Returns all the custom attributes that have been applied to the current , and that derive from a specified attribute type.
Docs.returns: An array that contains the custom attributes that are derived at any level from ; the array is empty if there are no such attributes.
Docs.exception: is .
is not a object supplied by the runtime. For example, is a object.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.Collections.Generic.IList`1<class System.Reflection.CustomAttributeData> GetCustomAttributesData() cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetCustomAttributesData () As IList(Of CustomAttributeData)

Docs.summary: Returns objects that contain information about the attributes that have been applied to the current .
Docs.returns: A generic list of objects representing data about the attributes that have been applied to the current module.

remarks.format: ## Remarks Use this method to examine the custom attributes of code in the reflection-only context, in cases where the custom attributes themselves are defined in code that is loaded into the reflection-only context. Methods such as <xref:System.Attribute.GetCustomAttributes*?displayProperty=nameWithType> and <xref:System.Reflection.Assembly.GetCustomAttributes*?displayProperty=nameWithType> cannot be used in such cases, because they create instances of the attributes. Code in the reflection-only context cannot be executed. For more information and for example code, see the <xref:System.Reflection.CustomAttributeData> class.

remarks.format: ## Remarks `name` should not include the path to the file.

The caller does not have the required permission.

Docs.summary: Loads the specified manifest resource from this assembly.

Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported.
The caller does not have the required permission.

MemberSignature.Value: public override System.IO.Stream? GetManifestResourceStream (Type type, string name);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.IO.Stream GetManifestResourceStream(class System.Type type, string name) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetManifestResourceStream (type As Type, name As String) As Stream
public: override System::IO::Stream ^ GetManifestResourceStream(Type ^ type, System::String ^ name);

Docs.param: The type whose namespace is used to scope the manifest resource name.
The name of the manifest resource being requested.
Docs.summary: Loads the specified manifest resource, scoped by the namespace of the specified type, from this assembly.
Docs.returns: A representing this manifest resource.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The returned <xref:System.IO.Stream> has its file pointer set to the beginning of the resource.
## Examples If the full name of `type` is "MyNameSpace.MyClasses" and `name` is "Dot", `GetManifestResourceStream` will search for a resource named MyNameSpace.Dot.

Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported.
The caller does not have the required permission.

Docs.returns: The module being requested, or if the module is not found.

Public Overrides Function GetName (copiedName As Boolean) As AssemblyName

Docs.param: to set the code base to the location of the assembly after it is shadow-copied; to set the code base to the original location.
Docs.summary: Gets the that was specified when the current dynamic assembly was created, and sets the code base as specified.
Docs.returns: The name of the dynamic assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.Reflection.AssemblyName[] GetReferencedAssemblies() cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetReferencedAssemblies () As AssemblyName()

Docs.summary: Gets an incomplete list of objects for the assemblies that are referenced by this .
Docs.returns: An array of assembly names for the referenced assemblies. This array is not a complete list.

remarks.format: ## Remarks This method does not return a complete list of referenced assemblies. For example, if you apply a custom attribute to the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder>, the assembly in which the attribute was defined is included in the list returned by this method. However, if you use a <xref:System.Type> object to specify the type of a method parameter, that type is not included.

Docs.summary: Gets a specified satellite assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.Reflection.Assembly GetSatelliteAssembly(class System.Globalization.CultureInfo culture) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetSatelliteAssembly (culture As CultureInfo) As Assembly

Docs.param: The specified culture.
Docs.summary: Gets the satellite assembly for the specified culture.
Docs.returns: The specified satellite assembly.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Satellite assemblies contain localized resources, as distinct from main application assemblies, which contain non-localizable executable code and resources for a single culture that serve as the default or neutral culture.
Call this method to use your current assembly version.

Docs.exception: is .
The assembly cannot be found.
The satellite assembly with a matching file name was found, but the did not match the one specified.
The satellite assembly is not a valid assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: public override System.Reflection.Assembly GetSatelliteAssembly (System.Globalization.CultureInfo culture, Version? version);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.Reflection.Assembly GetSatelliteAssembly(class System.Globalization.CultureInfo culture, class System.Version version) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetSatelliteAssembly (culture As CultureInfo, version As Version) As Assembly
public: override System::Reflection::Assembly ^ GetSatelliteAssembly(System::Globalization::CultureInfo ^ culture, Version ^ version);

Docs.param: The specified culture.
The version of the satellite assembly.
Docs.summary: Gets the specified version of the satellite assembly for the specified culture.
Docs.returns: The specified satellite assembly.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Satellite assemblies contain localized resources, as distinct from main application assemblies, which contain non-localizable executable code and resources for a single culture that serve as the default or neutral culture.
Call the <xref:System.Reflection.Assembly.GetSatelliteAssembly(System.Globalization.CultureInfo)> overload to use your current assembly version.

Docs.exception: is .
The satellite assembly with a matching file name was found, but the or the version did not match the one specified.
The assembly cannot be found.
The satellite assembly is not a valid assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: public override Type? GetType (string name, bool throwOnError, bool ignoreCase);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.Type GetType(string name, bool throwOnError, bool ignoreCase) cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetType (name As String, throwOnError As Boolean, ignoreCase As Boolean) As Type
override this.GetType : string * bool * bool -> Type
public: override Type ^ GetType(System::String ^ name, bool throwOnError, bool ignoreCase);

Attribute.AttributeName: [System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCode("Types might be removed by trimming. If the type name is a string literal, consider using Type.GetType instead.")]
[<System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.RequiresUnreferencedCode("Types might be removed by trimming. If the type name is a string literal, consider using Type.GetType instead.")>]

Docs.param: The name of the type to search for.
to throw an exception if the type is not found; otherwise, .
to ignore the case of the type name when searching; otherwise, .
Docs.summary: Gets the specified type from the types that have been defined and created in the current .
Docs.returns: The specified type, or if the type is not found or has not been created yet.

remarks.format: ## Remarks A type cannot be found until it has been created by calling the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.TypeBuilder.CreateType*> method.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property HostContext As Long
public: virtual property long HostContext { long get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the host context where the dynamic assembly is being created.
Docs.value: A value that indicates the host context where the dynamic assembly is being created.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property IsCollectible As Boolean
public: virtual property bool IsCollectible { bool get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether this dynamic assembly is held in a collectible .
Docs.value: if this dynamic assembly is held in a collectible ; otherwise, .

related.title: How to use and debug assembly unloadability

MemberSignature.Value: public override bool IsDefined (Type attributeType, bool inherit);
.method public hidebysig virtual instance bool IsDefined(class System.Type attributeType, bool inherit) cil managed
Public Overrides Function IsDefined (attributeType As Type, inherit As Boolean) As Boolean
public: override bool IsDefined(Type ^ attributeType, bool inherit);

Docs.param: The type of attribute to test for.
This argument is ignored for objects of this type.
Docs.summary: Returns a value that indicates whether one or more instances of the specified attribute type is applied to this member.
Docs.returns: if one or more instances of is applied to this dynamic assembly; otherwise, .

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property IsDynamic As Boolean
public: virtual property bool IsDynamic { bool get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a value that indicates that the current assembly is a dynamic assembly.

remarks.format: ## Remarks An <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder> object always represents a dynamic assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property Location As String
public: virtual property System::String ^ Location { System::String ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the location, in codebase format, of the loaded file that contains the manifest if it is not shadow-copied.
Docs.value: The location of the loaded file that contains the manifest. If the loaded file has been shadow-copied, the is that of the file before being shadow-copied.
Docs.exception: This method is not currently supported.
The caller does not have the required permission.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property ManifestModule As Module
public: virtual property System::Reflection::Module ^ ManifestModule { System::Reflection::Module ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the module in the current that contains the assembly manifest.

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property ReflectionOnly As Boolean
public: virtual property bool ReflectionOnly { bool get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the dynamic assembly is in the reflection-only context.
Docs.value: if the dynamic assembly is in the reflection-only context; otherwise, .

remarks.format:
## Remarks To emit a dynamic assembly in the reflection-only context, specify <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilderAccess.ReflectionOnly?displayProperty=nameWithType> when you create the <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder>. If a dynamic assembly is emitted in the reflection-only context, its code cannot be executed.
> [!NOTE] > After you have saved a dynamic assembly to disk, you can use the <xref:System.Reflection.Assembly.ReflectionOnlyLoad*?displayProperty=nameWithType> method to load the completed assembly into the reflection-only context. However, the assembly can no longer be modified.

Docs.related: How to: Load Assemblies into the Reflection-Only Context

Docs.summary: Saves this dynamic assembly to disk.
Sets a custom attribute on this assembly.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig instance void SetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder customBuilder) cil managed

Docs.param: An instance of a helper class to define the custom attribute.
Docs.summary: Set a custom attribute on this assembly using a custom attribute builder.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
> [!NOTE] > <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder.SetCustomAttribute*> cannot be used to set declarative security attributes. Use one of the overloads of <xref:System.AppDomain.DefineDynamicAssembly*> that takes required, optional, and refused permissions.
## Examples The following code sample illustrates the use of `SetCustomAttribute` within <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder>, using a <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.CustomAttributeBuilder>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/assemblybuilder_setcustomattribute1.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/assemblybuilder_setcustomattribute1.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception: is .
The caller does not have the required permission.

MemberSignature.Value: public void SetCustomAttribute (System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo con, byte[] binaryAttribute);
.method public hidebysig instance void SetCustomAttribute(class System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo con, unsigned int8[] binaryAttribute) cil managed
Public Sub SetCustomAttribute (con As ConstructorInfo, binaryAttribute As Byte())

Docs.param: The constructor for the custom attribute.
A byte blob representing the attributes.
Docs.summary: Set a custom attribute on this assembly using a specified custom attribute blob.

remarks.format:
## Remarks
For information on how to format `binaryAttribute`, see the metadata specification in Partition II of the [Common Language Infrastructure (CLI) specification](https://www.ecma-international.org/publications-and-standards/standards/ecma-335/).
`RuntimeConstructorInfo` is a special type generated by the system. It derives from the <xref:System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo> class, and any <xref:System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo> object you obtain through reflection is actually an instance of `RuntimeConstructorInfo`.
> [!NOTE] > <xref:System.Reflection.Emit.AssemblyBuilder.SetCustomAttribute*> cannot be used to set declarative security attributes. Use one of the overloads of <xref:System.AppDomain.DefineDynamicAssembly*> that takes required, optional, and refused permissions.
## Examples The following code sample illustrates the use of `SetCustomAttribute` to attach a custom attribute to a dynamically generated assembly.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/assemblybuilder_setcustomattribute2.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Reflection.Emit/AssemblyBuilder/SetCustomAttribute/assemblybuilder_setcustomattribute2.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception: or is .
The caller does not have the required permission.
is not a object.

MemberSignature.Value: protected abstract void SetCustomAttributeCore (System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo con, ReadOnlySpan<byte> binaryAttribute);
.method familyhidebysig newslot virtual instance void SetCustomAttributeCore(class System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo con, valuetype System.ReadOnlySpan`1<unsigned int8> binaryAttribute) cil managed
Protected MustOverride Sub SetCustomAttributeCore (con As ConstructorInfo, binaryAttribute As ReadOnlySpan(Of Byte))
abstract member SetCustomAttributeCore : System.Reflection.ConstructorInfo * ReadOnlySpan<byte> -> unit
protected: abstract void SetCustomAttributeCore(System::Reflection::ConstructorInfo ^ con, ReadOnlySpan<System::Byte> binaryAttribute);

Docs.param: The constructor for the custom attribute.
A of bytes representing the attribute.
Docs.summary: When overridden in a derived class, sets a custom attribute on this assembly.
Sets the entry point for this dynamic assembly.
